description_long: This is a bulbous, hand-crafted ceramic vase with a single rounded handle attached to its neck. The surface is adorned with bold, geometric patterns in dark brown and black, set against a reddish-brown clay background. The neck is short and slightly flared, leading to a wide, open mouth suitable for holding flowers or liquids. The body of the vase is spherical, tapering gently at the base, and shows signs of age with visible scuffs and worn patches. The handle is thick and robust, seamlessly integrated into the upper portion of the vessel. The overall finish is matte, with a slightly rough texture indicative of traditional pottery techniques.
description: A round, reddish-brown ceramic vase with geometric black patterns and a single sturdy handle, showing signs of age and wear.
description_view_0: The vase is seen from the front, highlighting the handle on the left and the bold geometric patterns on the body.
description_view_1: The back view displays the handle centered, with the geometric motifs wrapping around the vase's surface.
description_view_2: This side view shows the handle on the right and more of the worn, scuffed areas on the vase's body.
description_view_3: The opposite side reveals the vase without the handle visible, focusing on the continuous geometric patterns and surface wear.
